name: Sunday Morning Egg Beater Sourdough Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Nonfat Dry Milk Powder, optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the active starter, egg beaters, powdered milk, and brown sugar in a bowl until well combined.
2. Gradually add water or whole wheat flour until the batter reaches the desired consistency. Experiment to achieve your preferred thickness (some prefer it as thin as melted ice cream, while others like it thicker for slower pouring).
3. Stir in the baking soda and let the mixture stand for about one minute before cooking.
4. Heat a hot, oiled griddle (cast iron works best).
5. Pour batter onto the griddle, using an inverted spoon or spatula to spread into circles.
6. Cook until bubbles form on the surface of the pancake, then flip and cook the other side until golden brown.
Variations:
1. Add 1 cup of blueberries or other fruit before cooking.
2. Try adding 1/2 cup of buckwheat, cracked wheat, wheat germ, etc., to the batter.
3. Omit brown sugar and add 1 cup Granola for extra nutty flavor.
